name: dial-chat-model-provider
description: A VS Code chat model provider for DIAL API with OpenID Connect and API Key authentication

dial.authMethod.description: Authentication method: 'openid' for OpenID Connect or 'apikey' for API Key

dial.oidcClientId.description: OpenID Connect client ID (public identifier; auto-registered on login when empty)
dial.oidcClientId.markdownDescription: Public client ID from Keycloak. After dynamic registration this field is filled automatically. Use **DIAL: Clear OAuth Client** to reset an auto-registered client. Secrets (client secret, initial access token, API key) are never stored here — see commands **DIAL: Set OIDC Client Secret**, **DIAL: Set OIDC Initial Access Token**, **DIAL: Set API Key**.

dial.oidcScopes.description: Space-separated OIDC scopes for login and client registration (must include openid)
dial.oidcScopes.markdownDescription: Scopes for authorization and DCR. Include **`offline_access`** so Keycloak returns a refresh token (long-lived session). Also sent at DCR without `openid`.

dial.oauthCallbackPort.description: Local TCP port for OAuth redirect (loopback)
dial.oauthCallbackPort.markdownDescription: Redirect URI: `http://127.0.0.1:PORT/oauth-callback`. After changing, run **DIAL: Clear OAuth Client** and login again.

dial.oauthBrowserProfile.description: Browser profile for the OAuth sign-in window
dial.oauthBrowserProfile.markdownDescription: **auto** — use your Chrome/Edge profile when the browser is not already running (password autofill works). Falls back to a separate DIAL profile if Chrome/Edge is open. **system** — always use your main browser profile (close Chrome/Edge first). **persistent** — always use a separate DIAL profile (no saved passwords from your main browser).

dial.useServerTokenization.description: Use the DIAL /tokenize endpoint for accurate token counts
dial.useServerTokenization.markdownDescription: When enabled, the extension calls `/v1/deployments/{id}/tokenize` for `provideTokenCount` (results are cached by content hash). When disabled, token counting is unavailable — the IDE cannot show an accurate context budget.

dial.httpRetryMaxAttempts.description: Max retry attempts for transient DIAL HTTP failures
dial.httpRetryMaxAttempts.markdownDescription: How many times to retry a failed `/tokenize` or `/chat/completions` request when the error looks transient (503, connection reset, empty body, etc.). Semantic chat retries (unsupported `max_tokens`, context clamp) are separate and do not consume this budget.

dial.httpRetryBaseDelayMs.description: Initial backoff delay (ms) before the first HTTP retry

dial.httpRetryMaxDelayMs.description: Maximum backoff delay (ms) between HTTP retries

dial.chatStreamTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: How long to wait for the streaming `/chat/completions` response before giving up on one attempt. Large agent prompts may sit in the upstream queue for minutes — increase this (and `dial.httpRetryMaxDelayMs`) when logs show `(empty response body)` while vLLM is overloaded.

dial.embeddingsTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: How long to wait for a `/embeddings` response before giving up on one attempt. CPU embedding backends and large batches can take several minutes under load — increase this when `#codebase` search logs `timeout of 60000ms exceeded`.

dial.requiredTopics.markdownDescription: Show only models whose DIAL Admin **Topics** (API field `description_keywords`) include at least one of these tags. Leave empty to show all models returned by the server.

commands.title: DIAL: Set OIDC Initial Access Token

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.test:quick: npm run compile-tests && npm run lint && vscode-test
scripts.check: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run compile
